articles.title: Proposed CMS rule would set prior auth deadlines for drugs
articles.summary: The Trump administration has issued a proposed rule that aims to significantly overhaul prior authorization for pharmaceuticals.

articles.title: Epic rolls out health alerts to flag rising rates of illness at the county level
articles.summary: Electronic health record giant Epic debuted a new feature that monitors county-level health trends and issues alerts when elevated rates of illness are detected.

articles.title: Regulatory burdens continue to mount for physician practices
articles.summary: A new survey from MGMA outlines prior authorization, Medicare Advantage requirements and quality reporting are weighing heavily on medical groups, driving burnout and forcing practices to bring on more administrative staff.

articles.title: Amazon launches 2 new digital health partnerships for nutrition therapy, sleep care in health conditions program
articles.summary: Eligible Amazon customers will now be able to access benefits from AI-powered nutrition therapy provider Berry Street and virtual sleep clinic Dreem Health.

articles.title: Reliance on EHR vendors' tech roadmap slows down AI progress, senior IT leaders say
articles.summary: Despite reliance on EHR vendors for AI implementation, Qventus found senior IT leaders now want to move forward on AI deployment as only 22% of respondents said they would wait for an EHR feature, down from 52% in 2025.

articles.title: Orlando Health fleshes out Alabama footprint with another acquisition
articles.summary: The $10 billion nonprofit plans to acquire RMC Health System from the City of Anniston, building on 2024's big-ticket purchase of five Alabama hospitals from Tenet Healthcare.

articles.title: US adults still turn to providers for accurate health information even as AI chatbot use grows: Pew survey
articles.summary: Researchers surveyed more than 5,000 U.S. adults last October on where they seek out health information—and their views on how accurate results are.

articles.title: STAT+: FDA rejects an industry proposal to deregulate some AI devices
articles.summary: In this edition of Health Tech: FDA rejects a proposal to deregulate certain types of AI devices, pharma invests $50 million in DTx, and more.

articles.title: CMS unveils first wave of health tech tools as it pushes data sharing initiative
articles.summary: The agency showcased dozens of tools Thursday, about 8 months after the CMS first announced the initiative that aims to ease health data sharing and improve access to digital health and AI products.

articles.title: Most health AI users don’t rate chatbots as highly accurate: poll
articles.summary: About 2 in 10 said they at least sometimes use AI chatbots to get health information, but only 18% considered their responses highly accurate, according to the survey published by the Pew Research Center.

articles.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
articles.summary: In the early days of the war on Iran, while the White House was busy posting Call of Duty memes and AI slop of dancing bowling pins, the Iranian regime's state media was flooding the zone with video after video of what was happening on the ground: Explosions over Tehran. Smoke billowing in the sky. […]

articles.title: Gen Z’s love-hate relationship with AI
articles.summary: Gen Z is increasingly disillusioned with AI - just not enough to stop using it. A new Gallup report released this week, based on responses from nearly 1,600 people ages 14 to 29 across the US, suggests the hype is wearing off for the digital-native generation as AI becomes more embedded in school and work. articles.title: Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps
articles.summary: Microsoft is starting to remove "unnecessary" Copilot buttons from its Windows 11 apps. In the latest version of the Notepad app for Windows Insiders, Microsoft has removed the Copilot button in favor of a "writing tools" menu. The Copilot button in the Snipping Tool app also no longer appears when you select an area to […]

articles.title: Q&A: WestBridge Capital on AI-driven consolidation of power, startup survival
articles.summary: Kamal Singh, senior vice president at global investment firm WestBridge Capital, sat down with MobiHealthNews to discuss the potential for consolidation of power among select companies due to AI's impact in healthcare and how startups can ensure longevity in the space.

articles.title: HIMSSCast: The AI guiding principles of Elevance Health
articles.summary: Ratnakar Lavu, chief digital information officer at Elevance Health, discusses how the company is implementing AI into its workflow and the framework the health insurer uses to validate AI models, which prioritizes accountability, transparency, fairness, privacy and safety.  Lavu said the principles are designed to ensure AI systems are explainable, unbiased and continuously monitored over time. He also notes the importance of cross-functional collaboration between teams to evaluate AI

articles.title: Mayo Clinic enhances imaging test with AI
articles.summary:
Researchers at Rochester, Minn.-based Mayo Clinic used AI to determine that measuring fat around the heart greatly improves the accuracy of predicting long-term cardiovascular disease risk. The investigators applied AI to thousands of study participants’ standard coronary artery scans, finding that the volume of heart fat could forecast cardiovascular events, according to the research published […]

articles.title: CMS proposes extension of prior authorization rule to cover drugs: 6 notes
articles.summary:
CMS has proposed extending its prior authorization interoperability framework to cover drugs for the first time, building on its 2024 rule that focused on medical items and services. The proposed rule would also shorten decision timeframes and expand public reporting requirements. Six things to know: 1. CMS released the 2026 Interoperability Standards and Prior Authorization […]

articles.title: Penn Medicine, Epic lean into EHR ‘nudges’
articles.summary:
Philadelphia-based Penn Medicine is working with Epic to incorporate more “nudges” into the EHR to lead clinicians and patients to evidence-based choices for better health outcomes.
